description: Curated root-cause bug pattern seed pack. Patterns are priors for search and review, not proof.

patterns.title: Windows service stop handler does not signal worker shutdown
symptoms: sc stop does not stop the service
service remains running
service remains stop pending
process keeps running after stop request
root_causes: SERVICE_CONTROL_STOP handler returns without setting the shared stop event or cancellation flag
worker loop waits forever because it never observes the shutdown signal
out_of_range_cases: stopEvent is null
stop flag remains false after SERVICE_CONTROL_STOP
worker thread waits with INFINITE timeout
verification_probes: log entry and exit of SERVICE_CONTROL_STOP handler
assert SetEvent(stopEvent) or cancellation flag is reached
trace SetServiceStatus transitions to SERVICE_STOPPED

sources.evidence: Common fixed pattern in Windows service issue and PR threads; hydrate with github-search for project-specific citations.

patterns.title: Windows service status remains STOP_PENDING or RUNNING
symptoms: sc stop hangs
service control manager times out
service stuck stop pending
root_causes: service shutdown path does not call SetServiceStatus with SERVICE_STOPPED on every exit path
checkpoint and wait hint are updated without final stopped transition
out_of_range_cases: error path exits before SERVICE_STOPPED
stop pending loop increments checkpoint forever
worker error path skips final status update
verification_probes: trace every shutdown return path for SetServiceStatus(SERVICE_STOPPED)
inject worker startup failure and stop failure paths
capture SCM status transitions

sources.evidence: Common Windows service shutdown root-cause pattern.

patterns.title: Blocking worker call ignores service stop cancellation
symptoms: service cannot stop while waiting for IO
stop command returns timeout
background worker never exits
root_causes: worker thread blocks on a synchronous wait, socket, pipe, or device call that is not made cancelable by the stop path
shutdown flag is checked only before entering a blocking call
out_of_range_cases: INFINITE wait has no stop event in the wait set
CancelIoEx is never called for pending IO
shutdown flag changes while worker is blocked
verification_probes: trace pending IO count during stop
assert stop event participates in every blocking wait
force IO timeout and observe worker exit

sources.evidence: Common service and daemon cancellation pattern.

patterns.title: Driver pending IRP has no safe cancel path
symptoms: device close hangs
driver unload hangs
pending request never completes
user process blocks forever
root_causes: IRP is queued pending without a cancel routine or cancel-safe queue
cancel path races with completion path and leaves the IRP owned by neither side
out_of_range_cases: IRP canceled after being queued
cleanup runs while request remains pending
cancel routine pointer is null
verification_probes: force CancelIoEx from user mode while IRP is pending
log cancel routine install/remove ownership transitions
assert every pending IRP completes exactly once

sources.evidence: Common WDM/KMDF pending request root-cause pattern.

patterns.title: Driver runs paged or blocking code at elevated IRQL
symptoms: IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL bugcheck
page fault in nonpaged area
driver crashes under load
sporadic BSOD
root_causes: callback can run at DISPATCH_LEVEL but calls paged code, waits, allocates paged memory, or touches pageable state
IRQL precondition is assumed but not checked
out_of_range_cases: callback arrives at DISPATCH_LEVEL
spin lock is held while calling pageable helper
PagedPool allocation happens above PASSIVE_LEVEL
verification_probes: assert KeGetCurrentIrql before every blocking or paged helper
run verifier IRQL checking
mark callback path and pool type in traces

sources.evidence: Common Windows kernel crash root-cause pattern.

patterns.title: Driver unload misses outstanding reference cleanup
symptoms: driver cannot unload
device object remains referenced
verifier reports leaked reference
process protection callback remains installed
root_causes: unload path unregisters only part of callbacks/timers/work items
reference counted objects are not decremented on error or teardown path
out_of_range_cases: registration handle is overwritten or null at unload
work item can requeue after unload starts
reference count never reaches zero
verification_probes: trace every register/unregister pair
assert no work item/timer active after unload begins
run driver verifier pool and reference checks

sources.evidence: Common Windows kernel teardown pattern.

patterns.title: IOCTL handler trusts user buffer size or decoded length
symptoms: driver crashes on malformed ioctl
invalid user input corrupts state
IOCTL succeeds with truncated payload
root_causes: handler checks outer buffer length but trusts an embedded length/count/offset field
METHOD_NEITHER or pointer payload is used without probing and bounds validation
out_of_range_cases: embedded count exceeds InputBufferLength
offset plus size overflows
user pointer is null or invalid
verification_probes: fuzz IOCTL buffers with zero, short, huge, and overflowed lengths
assert decoded offsets fit the original buffer
trace rejected command codes

sources.evidence: Common driver fuzzing finding pattern.

patterns.title: CLFS log metadata parser trusts mutable BLF offsets, lengths, or object lifetime
symptoms: local privilege escalation through CLFS
clfs.sys crashes while opening or closing log files
SYSTEM privileges gained after crafted log metadata is processed
pool corruption or use-after-free in Common Log File System paths
root_causes: CLFS base log file metadata contains attacker-influenced offsets, counts, or size fields that are not validated against the decoded block bounds before allocation, copy, or memset operations
client, container, or security context metadata can corrupt persisted runtime pointers when symbol-zone and signature offsets are accepted out of range
log object lifetime is not protected across close, container update, metadata writeback, and cleanup paths, leaving stale CLFS objects reachable after free
out_of_range_cases: offset plus size wraps or lands outside the metadata block
symbol-zone length is accepted after a corrupted signature offset changes the validation window
container context stores or restores a kernel pointer from corrupted persisted metadata
close path releases a log object while another CLFS operation still has a reachable reference
verification_probes: fuzz CLFS BLF control, base, and truncate records with short, huge, wrapped, and cross-block offsets
assert every decoded offset and count is range-checked before pointer arithmetic
enable Special Pool, pool cookies, and Driver Verifier on CLFS-like metadata parsers
trace reference ownership across create, add container, write metadata, close, and cleanup paths

sources.evidence: MSRC classifies CVE-2025-29824 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege use-after-free with exploitation detected.
MSRC classifies CVE-2024-49138 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege heap-based buffer overflow with public disclosure and exploitation detected.
MSRC classifies CVE-2023-28252 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege heap-based buffer overflow with exploitation detected.
MSRC classifies CVE-2023-36424 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege out-of-bounds read.
MSRC classifies CVE-2023-38144 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege buffer over-read.
MSRC classifies CVE-2023-36900 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege integer overflow or wraparound.
MSRC classifies CVE-2023-23376 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege heap-based buffer overflow with exploitation detected.
MSRC lists CVE-2022-37969 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege vulnerability with public disclosure and exploitation detected.
Zscaler root-cause analysis ties CVE-2022-37969 to insufficient bounds checks on BLF metadata offsets that can corrupt CLFS container context state.
MSRC lists CVE-2022-35803 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege vulnerability.
MSRC lists CVE-2022-24521 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege vulnerability with exploitation detected.
MSRC lists CVE-2021-40467 as a CLFS elevation-of-privilege vulnerability.

patterns.title: AFD socket IOCTL path trusts endpoint context lifetime, pointers, or locked buffers
symptoms: local privilege escalation through afd.sys
socket operation crashes in AFD
network endpoint IOCTL corrupts pool memory
kernel dereferences user-influenced socket state
sensitive data remains reachable after locked-memory handling
root_causes: AFD IOCTL handlers accept user-controlled socket context, pointer, length, or count fields without a full capture, probe, lock, and bounds validation sequence
endpoint, buffer, or MDL lifetime is not reference-protected across asynchronous socket state transitions and cancel or close paths
locked pages or kernel buffers are reused, unlocked, or disclosed without clearing sensitive state and ownership metadata
out_of_range_cases: user pointer is dereferenced after validation but before capture
endpoint close races an outstanding poll, send, receive, or connect request
buffer count exceeds the captured IOCTL input length
locked memory contains stale kernel or cross-request data after failure cleanup
verification_probes: fuzz AFD-like IOCTLs with invalid pointers, stale handles, huge counts, zero lengths, and concurrent close or cancel
assert every METHOD_NEITHER pointer is captured or probed under SEH before use
trace endpoint reference ownership across async socket operations
scrub and unlock MDLs on every success and failure path

sources.evidence: MSRC classifies CVE-2024-38193 as an AFD elevation-of-privilege use-after-free with exploitation detected.
MSRC classifies CVE-2024-38141 as an AFD elevation-of-privilege use-after-free.
MSRC classifies CVE-2024-43563 as AFD sensitive data storage in improperly locked memory leading to elevation of privilege.
MSRC classifies CVE-2023-21768 as an AFD elevation-of-privilege untrusted pointer dereference.
MSRC classifies CVE-2023-28218 as an AFD elevation-of-privilege heap-based buffer overflow.
MSRC lists CVE-2022-30151 as an AFD elevation-of-privilege vulnerability.

patterns.title: Win32k object state changes during user-mode callback are not revalidated
symptoms: Win32k elevation of privilege
window or menu object type confusion
kernel GUI object is accessed with stale flags
object memory corruption after NtUser or GDI callback
sandbox escape through GUI subsystem
root_causes: win32k returns from a user-mode callback without revalidating tagWND, menu, class, or console flags that can change during reentrancy
kernel code interprets a field as an offset, pointer, handle, or extra-bytes buffer based on stale object type state
window traversal or object lookup uses a user-influenced field without verifying style, ownership, and queue membership invariants after callback return
out_of_range_cases: callback changes a window flag but caller continues with the old interpretation
WndExtra switches between user pointer and desktop-heap offset without a synchronized flag update
child window style or ID field is accepted without validating the returned tagWND
menu or window object is destroyed or retyped while still referenced by a traversal path
verification_probes: audit every win32k user-mode callback for state that must be rechecked after return
add invariant assertions for tagWND flags, object type, owner thread, and desktop heap range
fuzz NtUser window creation and menu paths with callback reentrancy enabled
run variant analysis for fields whose meaning depends on a mutable flag

sources.evidence: MSRC classifies CVE-2023-29336 as a Win32k elevation-of-privilege use-after-free with exploitation detected.
Project Zero describes CVE-2022-21882 as a win32k window object type confusion where object type state changed across a callback and was not rechecked.
Project Zero describes CVE-2021-1732 as win32k flag state going out of sync in the create-window path after a user-mode callback.
McAfee patch analysis ties CVE-2016-7255 to missing validation in win32k xxxNextWindow after user-influenced window state changes.
The CVE record describes CVE-2018-8453 as Win32k failing to properly handle objects in memory, resulting in elevation of privilege.
MSRC lists CVE-2022-21882 as a Win32k elevation-of-privilege vulnerability with exploitation detected.
MSRC lists CVE-2021-1732 as a Windows Win32k elevation-of-privilege vulnerability with exploitation detected.

patterns.title: Win32k graphics or composition path trusts command buffers, stack sizes, or shared pointers
symptoms: Win32k GRFX or ICOMP elevation of privilege
DWM or composition path dereferences an untrusted pointer
stack or heap overflow while processing graphics commands
graphics kernel path corrupts memory from user-driven composition state
root_causes: graphics or composition command decoding copies variable-sized regions to stack or heap buffers without checking element count, stride, or nested length fields
ICOMP or DWM shared state carries pointers or handles from a user-influenced process boundary without capture and revalidation
use-after-free in composition object lifetime allows stale surfaces, channels, or command objects to be reused
out_of_range_cases: clip or region count overflows allocation size
stack copy uses an attacker-controlled command length
shared composition pointer is freed or retyped before kernel use
DWM shared handle resolves to an object with unexpected type or owner
verification_probes: fuzz graphics and composition command streams with nested length mismatches
assert RtlSizeT arithmetic before every stack or heap copy
reference-check shared composition objects across channel teardown
validate object owner, type, and desktop session before dereferencing shared pointers

sources.evidence: MSRC classifies CVE-2024-30038 as a Win32K ICOMP elevation-of-privilege heap-based buffer overflow.
MSRC classifies CVE-2024-38246 as a Win32K GRFX elevation-of-privilege stack-based buffer overflow.
MSRC classifies CVE-2024-26241 as a Win32K ICOMP elevation-of-privilege use-after-free.
MSRC classifies CVE-2024-43636 as a DWM Core Library Win32k elevation-of-privilege untrusted pointer dereference.

patterns.title: Kernel Streaming driver updates shared filter or pin state without sufficient synchronization
symptoms: Kernel Streaming Service Driver elevation of privilege
race-dependent crash in audio or video streaming IOCTLs
filter, pin, event, or property state changes during concurrent close or cancel
shared KS object is used after teardown
root_causes: KSPROPERTY, KSMETHOD, or KSEVENT handling reads and writes shared filter, pin, allocator, or event state without holding the same lock across validation and use
IRP cancellation or handle close can race an in-flight streaming request and leave an object owned by neither path
reference count transitions are split from list insertion or removal for shared KS resources
out_of_range_cases: two threads change pin state while one request still uses old allocator framing
event list entry is removed during notification
cancel path completes an IRP while property path still references its context
close path frees a filter object before pending work drains
verification_probes: run concurrent property, method, event, close, and cancel fuzzing against KS-style devices
assert reference acquisition and list membership happen under one synchronization discipline
enable Driver Verifier deadlock, IRQL, pool, and I/O verification
trace lock ownership and object refcount across every streaming state transition

sources.evidence: MSRC classifies CVE-2024-38191 as a Kernel Streaming Service Driver elevation-of-privilege race condition.

patterns.title: Network stack parser uses wrapped length arithmetic for packet or ICS state
symptoms: remote code execution in tcpip.sys
local or network service privilege escalation from malformed network state
packet parser allocates too small a buffer
IPv6 or ICS path crashes on crafted lengths
root_causes: packet or network-service parser subtracts header, option, or payload lengths without proving the minuend is large enough
integer overflow or underflow converts a rejected packet length into a small allocation or large copy
fragment, extension-header, or connection-sharing metadata is trusted across layers without normalizing length and count invariants
out_of_range_cases: header length exceeds remaining packet length before subtraction
fragment reassembly length wraps below zero
option length plus header length overflows allocation size
ICS state transition trusts a count from a lower layer without bounds normalization
verification_probes: fuzz IPv6 extension-header and fragmentation paths with zero, tiny, maximum, and wrapped lengths
replace raw arithmetic with checked RtlSizeTAdd and RtlSizeTSub-style helpers
assert allocation size and copy length derive from the same validated normalized length
run packet parser tests under Driver Verifier and kernel memory sanitization where available

sources.evidence: MSRC classifies CVE-2024-38063 as a critical Windows TCP/IP remote code execution integer underflow.
MSRC classifies CVE-2023-35632 as a Windows Internet Connection Sharing elevation-of-privilege integer overflow or wraparound.

patterns.title: Party member count is stale after invite or kick churn
symptoms: party member limit can be bypassed
extra party member invited after kick
party size exceeds max members
root_causes: invite eligibility checks cached member count while kick/removal replication or backend confirmation is still pending
member count is decremented or incremented on only one of several invite/kick paths
out_of_range_cases: MemberCount differs from PartyMembers.Num()
PendingInvites are not counted against MaxMembers
kick pending state allows invite before removal commits
verification_probes: log MemberCount, PartyMembers.Num, PendingInvites for invite and kick events
assert invite guard uses authoritative committed plus pending count
run invite/kick/invite sequence on server authority

sources.evidence: Common multiplayer party state bug pattern.

patterns.title: Server RPC lacks authority or ownership validation
symptoms: client can perform unauthorized action
inventory or party state changes without permission
server accepts invalid client request
root_causes: Server RPC validates shape but not actor ownership, controller ownership, authority role, or current state
client-predicted state is trusted as authoritative
out_of_range_cases: requesting player does not own target actor
actor is not in expected gameplay state
validation returns true for stale or null owner
verification_probes: test RPC from non-owner client
assert server compares RequestingPlayer against authoritative owner
log role and owner for every state-mutating RPC

sources.evidence: Common Unreal multiplayer security root-cause pattern.

patterns.title: RepNotify order leaves dependent state inconsistent
symptoms: UI shows stale game state
client state flickers or becomes invalid
replicated value update is missed
root_causes: RepNotify handler assumes another replicated property has already arrived
dependent caches are rebuilt only in one OnRep path
out_of_range_cases: OnRep fires before initialization
dependent property still has old value
cache not invalidated when replicated array changes
verification_probes: trace OnRep ordering and dependent cache values
force packet loss/reorder in net emulation
assert cache rebuild occurs after every replicated source update

sources.evidence: Common replicated state ordering pattern.

patterns.title: Inventory or currency update double-spends across async callbacks
symptoms: item duplicated
currency spent twice
purchase succeeds twice
inventory count becomes negative
root_causes: client or server accepts repeated async completion without idempotency
pending transaction state is cleared too late or not bound to a unique request id
out_of_range_cases: same TransactionId is applied twice
ItemCount goes negative
callback arrives after cancel or retry
verification_probes: replay duplicate completion callback with same transaction id
assert balance transition is idempotent
log request id through start, retry, cancel, and completion

sources.evidence: Common game economy race pattern.

patterns.title: Client prediction lacks rollback for rejected server state
symptoms: client shows action success but server rejects it
state desync persists
movement or ability state diverges
root_causes: predicted local mutation is not rolled back when authoritative response rejects or corrects it
prediction key or sequence is not associated with the server correction
out_of_range_cases: server rejects action but predicted flag remains true
correction sequence is older than local prediction
rollback path does not restore dependent cache
verification_probes: force server rejection after local prediction
assert predicted state is cleared by correction
trace prediction key lifecycle

sources.evidence: Common multiplayer prediction consistency pattern.

patterns.title: UE project checkout is missing assets, plugins, submodules, or generated binaries
symptoms: project reports missing plugin
Content folder is absent
uasset appears corrupted after download
module is missing or built with a different engine version
GitHub source zip cannot be extracted or opened
root_causes: developer opened a GitHub ZIP or release source archive that omits submodules, Git LFS payloads, or private Content
plugin submodule paths are empty because the repo was cloned without --recurse-submodules or submodule URLs require a different protocol
Binaries and Intermediate are intentionally absent and the project must be regenerated and built locally
out_of_range_cases: plugin directory exists but contains no uplugin
uasset file is a Git LFS pointer or truncated archive member
Content is intentionally private or contains marketplace assets that cannot be redistributed
GitHub release Source code zip is used instead of git clone
verification_probes: run git submodule status --recursive and check for empty plugin directories
verify large uasset and umap files are real binary assets rather than LFS pointers
regenerate Visual Studio or Xcode project files and build modules before treating missing binary dialogs as source failures
confirm whether the repository intentionally excludes Content or marketplace assets

sources.evidence: Missing FootTrailsGenerator dependency was traced to empty plugin submodules and fixed by recursive submodule checkout and HTTPS submodule URLs.
A corrupted source archive report was resolved by using git clone instead of GitHub's generated source ZIP for a very large UE project.
The maintainer confirmed the public repo intentionally excludes Content because it contains external assets and internal game logic.
Missing module dialog was explained as expected because the GitHub project does not include binary builds.

patterns.title: UE engine or branch mismatch hits API and header drift
symptoms: project compiles on one UE version but fails on another
UnrealHeaderTool cannot find a class
deprecated API warning becomes compile failure
Visual Studio or Xcode reports missing Unreal symbols
branch named for one engine version fails in a newer editor
root_causes: project branch, .uproject EngineAssociation, and installed engine version are out of sync
engine upgrade renamed or moved APIs and headers such as UCameraShake, IsPendingKill, or platform time helpers
Unity builds or precompiled headers hide missing includes until non-unity or another compiler is used
out_of_range_cases: UE4 branch is compiled with UE5
Mac build includes a Windows-only platform type
deprecated helper was removed in the target engine
header is transitively available only under unity builds
verification_probes: compare .uproject EngineAssociation and README branch guidance against the installed editor
run a clean non-unity build on each supported platform
replace platform-specific helpers with engine-generic APIs where possible
audit UHT errors for renamed engine classes before changing game logic

sources.evidence: Compile failure on an older branch was traced to UCameraShake being renamed to UCameraShakeBase in newer project code.
Mac UE5.5 build failed because game code used FWindowsPlatformTime in cross-platform code.
The fix replaced Windows-specific timing with FPlatformTime so non-Windows builds compile.
IsPendingKill deprecation was called out as an engine-version compatibility issue requiring IsValid-style checks.

patterns.title: Platform packaging fails from config or asset assumptions
symptoms: Android package fails with missing drawable resource
Mac build or cook fails while Windows works
packaged build crashes after host or connect
mobile build runs but input or lighting is unusable
root_causes: platform-specific manifest, icon, dylib, or plugin configuration references assets that do not exist for that target
desktop input, lighting, scalability, or networking assumptions were not validated on mobile
packaged builds expose memory or asset loading paths that PIE did not execute
out_of_range_cases: manifest references @drawable/icon_round but the drawable is absent
virtual thumbstick or touch action mapping is missing
Mac uses a Windows-only code path
packaged host/connect path allocates or loads differently from editor play
verification_probes: run cook/package for every advertised target before release
validate Android manifest resource references against Build/Android assets
test host, connect, and first gameplay transition in packaged builds
run mobile builds with platform-specific input and scalability profiles

sources.evidence: Android packaging failed because the manifest referenced drawable/icon_round; the workaround removed the roundIcon manifest update.
Android support was possible but still had critical lighting, performance, and virtual thumbstick gaps.
Mac build problems were closed after project-side fixes and use of a native UE5.2 install.
A source-built UE5.5 packaged build crashed on Host or Connect, showing a packaged-only path not covered by editor startup.

patterns.title: Pooled Actor or Component is reused without a complete reset
symptoms: projectile pooling crashes in Reset
pooled actor keeps stale state
pooled KD tree node corrupts spatial queries
component initializes twice or is uninitialized unexpectedly
widget or actor is recreated on every input press
root_causes: pool return path does not fully undo transient state, delegates, owner references, movement state, or initialized flags
reuse path calls InitializeComponent without checking whether the component is already initialized
custom pooled data structures do not provide a forced reset contract for every object type
out_of_range_cases: same component enters pool while still active
delegate remains bound after return to pool
pooled node still points to an old tree branch
input toggles create duplicate widgets instead of reusing an existing one
verification_probes: stress spawn and return objects for thousands of iterations
assert every pooled object has ResetForPool and ActivateFromPool coverage
trace component initialized and uninitialized transitions
check for duplicate widgets, bound delegates, and stale owner pointers after reuse

sources.evidence: Projectile pooling could crash in USProjectileMovementComponent::Reset; the report proposed guarding initialization with HasBeenInitialized and uninitialization.
Argus tracked a KD tree bug caused by node pooling and the need for a generalized forced reset interface.
Actor pooling design centered on mapping UClass to queues of empty instances, making class-specific reset behavior important.
A widget was recreated on every key press even when already visible, a UI-side pooling/lifecycle variant.

patterns.title: UI, pause, and input mode state is not restored or gated
symptoms: widget only appears in Simulate mode
game stays paused after calling SetGamePaused false
camera pans while hovering UI
left mouse click is unresponsive
UI freezes after starting gameplay
root_causes: widget creation runs with the wrong owning player, viewport, input mode, or game-paused tick policy
pause and input-mode transitions are not centralized and leave PlayerController, cursor capture, focus, or time dilation in an inconsistent state
gameplay input is not gated against UI hover/focus state
out_of_range_cases: widget has no owning local player
pause menu ticks disabled while paused
old UI-only input mode remains after closing menu
camera or click command ignores Slate hover state
verification_probes: log widget owner, viewport add, focus, cursor, and input mode for every menu transition
test PIE, Standalone, packaged build, and Simulate separately
assert a single source of truth for pause and active menu state
block gameplay camera or command input while pointer is over interactive UI

sources.evidence: Widget Blueprint appeared only in Simulate mode despite CreateWidget and AddToViewport attempts.
Game remained frozen even after SetGamePaused false, pointing at broader pause/input lifecycle state.
Camera panning needed to be prevented while hovering UI elements.
Open report describes unresponsive left click on PC and UI freeze after starting gameplay on Android.

patterns.title: GAS ability state leaks across instances, scanner types, or cooked cue discovery
symptoms: canceling a non-instanced ability is unreliable
cooldown blocks all ability variants instead of the active type
GameplayCue works in editor but not packaged
switching ability variants leaves altered visual state
ability activation failure needs a gameplay-visible delegate
root_causes: ability-wide state is used where the design requires per ability spec, per source object, or per scanner type state
non-instanced abilities have no lightweight active-operation record to cancel their own montage or effect
GameplayCue blueprint classes are discovered indirectly and are not cooked unless explicitly referenced or included
out_of_range_cases: one cooldown tag applies to every scanner type
ability switch occurs while a visual effect is shrinking back to default
packaged build omits a cue blueprint because no hard reference exists
cancel path cannot identify which non-instanced ability action is active
verification_probes: activate two ability variants and assert cooldowns are tracked per intended scope
package a build and verify GameplayCue classes are included in the cooked asset set
track active non-instanced ability operations with handles that can be canceled
switch variants during effect rollback and assert visual state returns to the new variant baseline

sources.evidence: The project recorded Epic's caveat that canceling a non-instanced ability may require a lightweight active ability record.
GameplayCue limitations included single listener behavior, cooked asset inclusion, and editor discovery restart caveats.
Ability-wide scan cooldown incorrectly disabled every scanner type and needed ScannerType-specific cooldowns.
Switching scanners while a beam-width ability was shrinking left the new scanner height wrong.

patterns.title: Data-driven UE records use hard references or late asset resolution
symptoms: spawn definitions are duplicated across code and Blueprints
static data records cannot be loaded safely at runtime
ability overrides are missing until first use
data table entries cannot drive valid enum-like choices
raw asset pointers break editor or packaged behavior
root_causes: gameplay definitions are hard-coded or stored as raw UObject pointers instead of data assets, soft object/class references, or stable record IDs
runtime systems resolve assets lazily without an async preload or cache path
editor-facing data lacks safe ID serialization and validation at PostEditChangeProperty time
out_of_range_cases: record stores a full object reference that is not cooked
record ID is assigned only at runtime and changes between sessions
ability override is requested before async preload completes
Blueprint dropdown points at stale data
verification_probes: load every data record in a cooked build before gameplay starts
validate soft references and record IDs in editor and CI
trace async preload completion before systems consume overrides
replace hard-coded class references with data-driven specs where designers need variation

sources.evidence: Argus converted entity templates from raw pointers to soft pointers.
Static data needed an Asset Loading Component for async pre-loading of records.
Ability overrides needed async preload before use.
ScanFun centralized scannable spawn definitions into a single data table or asset-driven place.

patterns.title: Spatial query uses center points or wrong boundaries instead of full extents
symptoms: large-radius entities clip or are missed by range queries
KD tree range search follows the wrong branch
surface spawning embeds meshes in the floor or floats them
fog or rasterization misses the right edge of a triangle
revealed area does not cover the full playable space
root_causes: query compares only actor or node centers and ignores radius, bounds, pivot, or mesh origin
inclusive/exclusive bounds are inconsistent at polygon or raster edges
KD tree pruning uses branch decisions that are valid for points but not for extents
asset origin offsets are assumed to be normalized
out_of_range_cases: radius overlaps query box but center lies outside
mesh origin is centered while placement expects bottom pivot
triangle pixel at the right extreme is skipped
obstacle edge segment is longer than KD tree assumptions
verification_probes: seed entities whose radius overlaps a query range while centers are outside
test min and max boundary pixels for rasterization
spawn meshes with different pivots and bounds on the same surface
visualize node bounds, query extents, and accepted/rejected candidates

sources.evidence: KD tree range query branch choice needed correction when finding nodes within range.
KD tree queries had to account for entity radius rather than only entity center points.
Node radius consideration was added to overlap and range checks to prevent clipping with large entities.
On-surface spawning broke for meshes with inconsistent origin offsets even though the code worked for normalized default cube assets.

patterns.title: ORCA or flocking avoidance oscillates from group, obstacle, or coordinate mismatch
symptoms: units orbit forever around the target
group members split into different avoidance groups
trailing unit oscillates
entities body-block at the stable range boundary
units face tiny movement directions or jitter
root_causes: avoidance group membership resets or splits when a new move command or waypoint starts
ORCA line generation uses the wrong coordinate handedness, obstacle offset, effort coefficient, or time horizon
same-group entities and construction entities are not excluded or weighted differently from true blockers
tiny movement vectors drive rotation and avoidance when they should be ignored
out_of_range_cases: same selected group generates full avoidance responsibility against itself
new move command clears group identity too early
left-handed and right-handed calculations are mixed
obstacle points are exactly on the navmesh edge and create unstable constraints
verification_probes: record avoidance group id across path completion and new command start
visualize ORCA lines, obstacle points, and chosen velocity every frame
run deterministic tests for center-intersection, long-segment, trailing, and cornering cases
clamp rotation and velocity updates below a tiny movement epsilon

sources.evidence: Argus tracked a perpetual orbiting issue in avoidance.
Avoidance had to be converted from left-handed to right-handed coordinates for ORCA.
Starting a new move command reset avoidance groups incorrectly.
The fix checklist covered group splits at waypoints, clumped group oscillation, same-group ORCA range reduction, target-within-own-group splits, and obstacle point offsets.
Open trailing oscillation issue suggests effort coefficient scaling by distance as a likely correction axis.

patterns.title: ECS or gameplay queries keep dead, passenger, or inactive entities in active systems
symptoms: dead actors can still be selected
dead entities remain in avoidance groups
reticle stays after casting entity dies
passengers are considered nearby blockers or targets
combat state requires extra input before movement resumes
root_causes: range, selection, targeting, reticle, and avoidance queries do not share a lifecycle predicate for alive, active, controllable, and visible entities
component removal or death state does not invalidate cached groups, reticles, passengers, or target references
state transitions from combat, garrison, death, and ability casting leave stale membership in ECS sets
out_of_range_cases: entity dies while reticle ability is active
passenger remains in spatial partitioning as a normal blocker
dead entity remains selected after destruction
combat target is cleared but movement task still waits for old combat state
verification_probes: apply the same alive and active predicate to every spatial and gameplay query
kill or garrison entities while selected, targeted, moving, and casting
assert death removes or invalidates reticles, targets, groups, and UI entries
unit-test component masks after DestroyEntity and RemoveComponentsForEntity

sources.evidence: Assorted bugs included dead entities in avoidance groups, selecting dead actors, and movement requiring a double-click after combat.
Garrison flow needed range predicates that ignore passengers and dead entities.
Reticle ability needed to disappear when the casting entity dies.
Reticle components had a bug around respecting one reticle per cast.

patterns.title: Fog-of-war raster visibility misses bounds, obstacles, or update throttling cases
symptoms: revealed area does not cover the full playable space
fog ignores obstacles
blurred reveal edge artifacts appear
fog updates are too slow
right-edge pixels are missing from revealed triangles
root_causes: visibility rasterization has off-by-one or triangle-boundary errors
line-of-sight obstacle intersection is calculated at the exact obstacle point without a stabilizing offset
active reveal updates are throttled without considering nearby moving allies
CPU blur and decay smoothing are not batched or parallelized for the reveal workload
out_of_range_cases: triangle right extreme is excluded
reveal exits early while a nearby ally is moving
obstacle intersection lies exactly on an edge and flickers
full playable extent is larger than the reveal buffer assumptions
verification_probes: snapshot fog buffers for min and max playable bounds
unit-test triangle and circle rasterization edge pixels
compare CPU and GPU blur output for the same alpha field
simulate moving allies near reveal boundaries and obstacles

sources.evidence: Fog updates needed to be constrained based on whether entities or allies were moving nearby.
Fog of war needed to respect obstacles for reveal state.
Triangle rasterization needed a bugfix at the right extreme of bounds.
TeamCommander revealed areas failed to cover the full extent of playable space.

patterns.title: Hit or overlap metric is read before the callback state is valid
symptoms: impact distance is always zero
debugger shows garbage values during overlap handling
bullet hole visibility distance is wrong
hit scoring differs from visual impact location
root_causes: distance or hit-derived state is inspected before assignment or before the overlap call stack has completed
impact point, component center, and target actor origin are mixed without validating which coordinate each value represents
debug-only observation happens before the code path that initializes the metric
out_of_range_cases: distance is read before the assignment statement executes
component location equals impact point because the wrong component is used
target center is uninitialized or still default
debugger watches a variable before the callback branch initializes it
verification_probes: log impact point, target center, component name, and assigned distance after the branch that computes them
initialize hit metrics to sentinel values and assert they are overwritten before use
draw debug impact and center positions in the same coordinate space
test direct center hit and edge hit cases

sources.evidence: Distance appeared as zero or garbage in the debugger until checked after initialization in the overlap branch.
Bullet hole visibility distance was tracked as a related hit-distance visibility issue.

patterns.title: Backend side effect lacks idempotency key
symptoms: duplicate order
duplicate payment
duplicate email
retry creates two records
same request applied twice
root_causes: handler performs side effects before checking or storing an idempotency key
retry path has no unique request key or unique constraint
out_of_range_cases: same request id reaches handler twice
network timeout causes client retry after commit
unique key is not enforced at database boundary
verification_probes: send duplicate POST with same idempotency key
simulate timeout after DB commit
assert unique constraint rejects duplicate transaction id

sources.evidence: Common web/backend retry root-cause pattern.

patterns.title: Stale authorization cache permits revoked access
symptoms: revoked user can still access resource
permission change is delayed
unauthorized action succeeds after role removal
root_causes: authorization decision is cached without invalidation on role or ownership change
cached subject/resource tuple omits tenant, scope, or version
out_of_range_cases: roleVersion changes but cache key stays the same
tenant id missing from cache key
permission revoked before TTL expiry
verification_probes: revoke role and retry request before cache TTL
assert cache key includes tenant, scope, and version
trace cache hit after permission mutation

sources.evidence: Common authorization caching bug pattern.

patterns.title: Transaction reads stale invariant before concurrent update
symptoms: limit exceeded under concurrent requests
two users claim same slot
balance becomes negative
quota bypass under load
root_causes: code checks invariant outside a serializable transaction or without row lock
read-modify-write sequence is not atomic
out_of_range_cases: two transactions read the same count then both insert
updatedRows is zero but success is returned
limit is checked before lock acquisition
verification_probes: run two concurrent requests against same resource
assert invariant check and write happen under the same lock/transaction
verify affected row count

sources.evidence: Common backend concurrency root-cause pattern.

patterns.title: Pagination cursor boundary skips or duplicates records
symptoms: pagination returns duplicate rows
last item missing
infinite next page
cursor skips records
root_causes: cursor comparison uses inclusive boundary where exclusive is required
sort key is not stable or omits tie-breaker id
cursor encodes only timestamp while multiple rows share it
out_of_range_cases: two records have same timestamp
cursor item is included again
page size exactly equals limit
verification_probes: seed duplicate timestamps around page boundary
assert order includes stable tie-breaker
request consecutive pages and check unique ids

sources.evidence: Common API pagination boundary pattern.

patterns.title: Frontend async handler uses stale closure state
symptoms: button uses old value
UI reverts after async call
selection or count becomes wrong
state update is lost
root_causes: async callback captures stale state value instead of using functional update or current ref
effect dependency array omits a variable used by the callback
out_of_range_cases: two updates race and second applies old state
effect runs with stale selectedId
callback resolves after component state changed
verification_probes: trigger two rapid UI actions before async completion
assert functional state update is used for dependent updates
enable exhaustive-deps lint for effect

sources.evidence: Common frontend state management root-cause pattern.

patterns.title: Optimistic UI update has no rejection rollback
symptoms: UI shows success after server rejection
client state differs from backend
deleted item reappears or remains deleted incorrectly
root_causes: optimistic mutation updates local cache but error path does not revert or refetch
request identity is not checked before applying success or failure callback
out_of_range_cases: server rejects request but local cache remains mutated
older mutation response overwrites newer state
rollback snapshot is missing
verification_probes: force API rejection after optimistic update
assert rollback snapshot is restored
race two mutations and check mutation id ordering

sources.evidence: Common frontend optimistic state pattern.

patterns.title: Go CLI agent misclassifies document-generation request as read-only
symptoms: agent does not create requested file
requested document is missing
assistant reports done without artifact
root_causes: intent classifier marks planning or analysis as read-only and suppresses write-capable tools
completion path does not assert expected artifact existence before final response
out_of_range_cases: prompt asks to create a file but readOnlyAnalysis is true
write tool is removed from allowed tool list
final response skips artifact existence check
verification_probes: trace intent classification and allowed tools for document requests
assert artifact path exists before final answer
unit test create-file prompt against tool routing

sources.evidence: Common coding-agent artifact routing pattern.

patterns.title: Context compaction drops required output artifact state
symptoms: agent forgets to generate a requested file
long-running task final answer omits artifact
resume loses pending output requirement
root_causes: compaction summary preserves discussion but not required file paths or pending artifact checklist
resume path trusts old plan status without revalidating filesystem outputs
out_of_range_cases: pending artifact list is empty after compaction
plan says completed but file does not exist
resume loads summary without output path
verification_probes: compact after requesting a file and verify pending artifact survives
resume and assert file existence before final answer
persist artifact checklist in session state

sources.evidence: Common long-context agent workflow pattern.
